architecture.summary: Follow existing block endpoint pattern: KV shard removal before DB delete, using updateSearchShard(kv, entry, 'remove'). Add TTL to all KV shard writes (7 days). Existence validation only for edge-only search results via batch IN query.

integrations.summary: Cloudflare KV (SEARCH_CACHE_KV) for search index shards. Postgres via Prisma for skill records. resolveEnv() for KV access. QueueKV interface may need expirationTtl support in put options.

ui-ux.summary: No UI changes. Fix is server-side only. Users see correct search results without 404 links. CLI (vskill find) unchanged -- server filters stale entries before response.

performance.summary: Existence validation uses single batch SELECT name FROM Skill WHERE name IN (...) query, under 50ms for 200 names. Only runs for edge-only results (rare path when Postgres is down). TTL refresh on daily rebuild prevents expiry under normal operation.

security.summary: No new auth surfaces. Delete endpoint already requires admin role or internal auth key. KV cleanup is best-effort -- failure doesn't leak data, just delays stale entry removal.

edge-cases.summary: KV unavailable during delete: DB deletion proceeds, TTL handles cleanup within 7 days. Skill already removed from KV: updateSearchShard remove is idempotent. Concurrent delete+search: TTL provides eventual consistency. Special chars in skill names: getNameShardKeys() handles slashes/hyphens. Full rebuild between delete and TTL: rebuild queries Postgres, skips deleted skills.
